title.en: Normative References: The Documents Behind the Document
title.zh: 规范性引用文件：标准背后的标准

blocks.en: IEEE 802.11 does not try to redefine everything from scratch. When it needs AES encryption, a hash function, an EAP method, or the rules for a country code, it points at a document that already specifies that thing and says, in effect, "use that." Clause 2 — Normative references — is the list of those external documents. The opening sentence is blunt about what that means: every document in the list is "indispensable for the application of this standard." In other words, wherever the main 802.11 text cites one of these, the requirement you must obey actually lives in the other document.
blocks.zh: IEEE 802.11 并不打算把所有东西都从零重新定义。当它需要 AES 加密、一个哈希函数、一种 EAP 方法，或者国家代码的规则时，它会指向一份已经规定了这些内容的文档，并实质上说一句：“照那个来。”第 2 章——规范性引用文件（Normative references）——就是这些外部文档的清单。开头那句话毫不含糊地说明了它的含义：清单中的每一份文档对“本标准的应用都是必不可少的（indispensable）”。换句话说，凡是 802.11 正文引用到其中某一份的地方，你真正必须遵守的要求其实写在那份外部文档里。

blocks.en: This matters for how you read the standard. A clause in 802.11 might say only one line — "keys are derived per RFC 5869" or "the cipher is AES as defined in FIPS 197" — and the substance of the requirement is entirely in the referenced spec. If you read only the 802.11 sentence and guess at the rest, you will get it wrong. The skill being taught here is recognising that hand-off: spotting when a requirement has been delegated to another document, and then chasing the citation out to the real source instead of inventing what you think it probably says.
blocks.zh: 这一点直接影响你阅读标准的方式。802.11 中的某个条款可能只写了一句话——“密钥按 RFC 5869 派生”或“密码算法为 FIPS 197 所定义的 AES”——而这条要求的实质内容完全在被引用的规范里。如果你只读 802.11 的那一句、再凭猜测补上其余部分，就一定会出错。本课要教的本领，正是识别这种“交接”：发现某条要求已被委托给另一份文档，然后顺着引用追溯到真正的源头，而不是凭空臆造你以为它“大概是这么说的”内容。

blocks.en: Normative vs informative is the line to keep clear. Clause 2 is normative — binding, you must comply. Annex A (the bibliography) is informative — background reading only, cited with bracketed numbers like [B18], and nothing there imposes a requirement. If a citation sends you to a bracketed [Bnn] entry, it is context, not a rule. If it names a document by title in Clause 2, it is a rule.
blocks.zh: “规范性”与“资料性”之间的界线务必分清。第 2 章是规范性的——具有约束力，你必须遵守。附录 A（参考文献 / bibliography）是资料性的——仅供背景阅读，用形如 [B18] 的方括号编号引用，其中任何内容都不构成要求。如果某处引用把你指向一个带方括号的 [Bnn] 条目，那是背景，不是规则；如果它在第 2 章中以文档标题点名引用，那才是规则。

blocks.en: The list itself is a map of where 802.11 borrows from. You will see other IEEE 802 standards (802, 802.1Q bridging, 802.3 Ethernet), ISO/IEC documents (the OSI reference model, ISO 3166 country codes, ASN.1), a long run of IETF RFCs (EAP in RFC 3748, EAP-TLS, IPv6, the HKDF key-derivation function in RFC 5869), FIPS crypto specs (FIPS 197 AES, FIPS 180-4 SHA), NIST Special Publications (GCM in SP 800-38D, key management in SP 800-57), and regulatory texts (ETSI EN 301 893 for 5 GHz operation in Europe, 3GPP for cellular interworking). When 802.11 talks about security, addressing, or radio rules, this is the shelf it is reaching to.
blocks.zh: 这份清单本身就是一张“802.11 向何处借用”的地图。你会看到其他 IEEE 802 标准（802、802.1Q 桥接、802.3 以太网）、ISO/IEC 文档（OSI 参考模型、ISO 3166 国家代码、ASN.1）、一长串 IETF RFC（RFC 3748 的 EAP、EAP-TLS、IPv6、RFC 5869 的 HKDF 密钥派生函数）、FIPS 密码规范（FIPS 197 AES、FIPS 180-4 SHA）、NIST 特别出版物（SP 800-38D 的 GCM、SP 800-57 的密钥管理），以及监管性文本（欧洲 5 GHz 运行的 ETSI EN 301 893、用于蜂窝互通的 3GPP）。当 802.11 谈到安全、寻址或无线电规则时，它伸手去取的就是这一书架上的文档。

blocks.en: Watch the dates. Clause 2 distinguishes dated from undated references. A dated reference ("FIPS 186-4", "IEEE Std 802.3-2018") pins you to exactly that edition — later editions do not apply. An undated reference ("IEEE Std 802.1Q", "ISO 639") means you follow the latest edition, including amendments and corrigenda, that existed when this standard was published. So when you chase a citation, match the edition the way the reference is written, or you may read a requirement that 802.11 never adopted.
blocks.zh: 注意版本日期。第 2 章区分了“带日期（dated）”与“不带日期（undated）”的引用。带日期的引用（如“FIPS 186-4”“IEEE Std 802.3-2018”）把你锁定在恰好那一版——更晚的版本不适用。不带日期的引用（如“IEEE Std 802.1Q”“ISO 639”）则表示你应采用本标准发布时存在的最新版本，包括其修正案与勘误。因此在追溯引用时，要按引用书写的方式去对应版本，否则你可能读到一条 802.11 从未采纳的要求。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 420 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' font-family='sans-serif' font-size='12'><rect x='10' y='70' width='150' height='60' rx='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='85' y='95' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor'>802.11 main text</text><text x='85' y='113' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>"...as defined in FIPS 197"</text><line x1='160' y1='100' x2='250' y2='100' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' marker-end='url(#a)'/><text x='205' y='92' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>Clause 2 lookup</text><rect x='250' y='40' width='160' height='44' rx='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='330' y='58' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>NORMATIVE (binding)</text><text x='330' y='74' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>FIPS 197 = real rule</text><rect x='250' y='118' width='160' height='44' rx='6' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><text x='330' y='136' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>INFORMATIVE (Annex A)</text><text x='330' y='152' text-anchor='middle' fill='currentColor' font-size='10'>[Bnn] = background</text><defs><marker id='a' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='7' refY='4' orient='auto'><path d='M0,0 L8,4 L0,8 z' fill='currentColor'/></marker></defs></svg>

caption.en: A one-line citation in the main text delegates the real requirement out to a Clause 2 document (binding); a bracketed [Bnn] would only point at Annex A background.
caption.zh: 正文中的一句引用，会把真正的要求委托给第 2 章中的某份文档（具约束力）；而方括号 [Bnn] 仅指向附录 A 的背景资料。

summary.en: What the Clause 2 boilerplate actually says — and why every entry is mandatory
summary.zh: 第 2 章开头的固定措辞究竟说了什么——以及为何每个条目都是强制的

blocks.en: The clause opens with the standard IEEE boilerplate: the referenced documents are "indispensable for the application of this standard (i.e., they must be understood and used; therefore, each referenced document is cited in the text and its relationship to this document is explained)." Two practical rules follow immediately. First, "For dated references, only the edition cited applies." Second, "For undated references, the latest edition of the referenced document (including any amendments or corrigenda) at the time of publication of this standard applies." The list that follows is alphabetised by source body and spans 3GPP, ETSI, FIPS, IANA, IEC, IEEE 802-family standards, the IETF RFC series, ISO and ISO/IEC, ITU-T, NIST Special Publications, OASIS, and OMA — each with a footnote telling you where to obtain the document. Note the deliberate contrast with Annex A: bracketed numbers such as [B18] (used, for example, alongside ISO/IEC 11802-5:1997 to cross-reference the former IEEE Std 802.1H) point into the informative bibliography, which is explicitly NOT binding. Reading well means honouring that split — comply with what Clause 2 names, treat [Bnn] entries as context.
blocks.zh: 本章以 IEEE 的标准固定措辞开篇：所列引用文档“对本标准的应用是必不可少的（即必须被理解并使用；因此每份引用文档都在正文中被引用，并说明了它与本文档的关系）”。紧接着给出两条实用规则。第一，“对于带日期的引用，仅所引用的那一版适用。”第二，“对于不带日期的引用，适用本标准发布时该引用文档的最新版本（包括任何修正案或勘误）。”随后的清单按来源机构的字母顺序排列，涵盖 3GPP、ETSI、FIPS、IANA、IEC、IEEE 802 系列标准、IETF RFC 系列、ISO 与 ISO/IEC、ITU-T、NIST 特别出版物、OASIS 以及 OMA——每一类都附有脚注，告诉你到哪里获取该文档。请注意它与附录 A 的刻意对照：诸如 [B18] 这样的方括号编号（例如与 ISO/IEC 11802-5:1997 并列出现，用以交叉引用原 IEEE Std 802.1H）指向的是资料性参考文献，而那部分明确不具约束力。读得好，意味着尊重这道分界——遵守第 2 章点名的内容，把 [Bnn] 条目当作背景。

cites.note: Clause 2 Normative references — 'indispensable' boilerplate; dated vs undated rule; opening entries (3GPP, ETSI, FIPS, IEEE 802 family)
Clause 2 continued — IETF RFC series, ISO/IEC, ITU-T, NIST SP, OASIS, OMA entries; [B18] cross-reference into Annex A informative bibliography
